title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: As urban populations continue to grow, city planners are increasingly turning to behavioural science to understand how people interact with public spaces. Early approaches (0) urban design as a purely technical discipline, but modern research highlights the importance of psychological factors.||One key insight is that individuals are more likely to (1) in pro‑environmental behaviour when the environment subtly encourages it. For example, placing recycling bins in highly visible locations can (2) a significant increase in correct waste disposal. Similarly, the layout of pedestrian areas can either (3) or discourage walking, depending on how intuitive the pathways feel.||Another important factor is social influence. People tend to (4) the behaviour of those around them, especially in ambiguous situations. This tendency can be used to (5) positive habits, such as energy conservation or reduced littering.||However, critics warn that such strategies may (6) ethical concerns if they manipulate behaviour without individuals’ awareness. Supporters argue that these interventions merely (7) existing tendencies rather than impose new ones. Ultimately, understanding how people respond to subtle cues may help cities (8) more sustainable and human‑centred environments.

example.explanation: The correct expression is “regarded urban design as…”. “Framed” might work in other contexts, but it's not the typical placement here; “enacted” and “appointed” are not used with disciplines.

questions.explanation: The intended answer is 'engage in behavior'. The others do not form natural placements with 'behavior'.
“Trigger an increase” is the standard placement. The others do not fit the meaning.
The original answer is 'hinder or encourage walking'. 'Foster' does not fit the contrast of the text; the others do not work with 'walking'.
The expression is 'mirror the behavior of others'. The others do not convey the idea of ​​imitation.
The placement is 'foster positive habits'. 'Enforce' implies obligation, which contradicts the focus of the text.
The expression is 'raise ethical concerns'. The others don't work with 'concerns'.
The intended answer is 'reinforce existing trends'. The others don't fit semantically.
The placement is 'foster positive habits'. 'Enforce' implies obligation, which contradicts the focus of the text.
